crime.value: Theft involving the Agra treasure and pursuit of suspects via steam launch
crime.quote: witness the way in which she preserved that Agra plan from all the other papers of her father

victim.value: Miss Morstan's father, who possessed the Agra plan/treasure documents
victim.quote: she preserved that Agra plan from all the other papers of her father

closed_world.value: River Thames and connected waterways where the Aurora launch operates
closed_world.quote: I am going down the river; and if I should see anything of the Aurora I shall let him know

culprit_and_motive.value: Wooden-legged man and confederate Lal Rao the butler, motive unclear from excerpt
culprit_and_motive.quote: a confederate in the house, who could be none other than Lal Rao, the butler

resolution.value: Holmes solved the case through deduction, Jones gets official credit for arrests
resolution.quote: Jones actually has the undivided honor of having caught one fish in his great haul

investigator.value: Sherlock Holmes as consulting detective, with Watson as narrator and participant
investigator.quote: I shall have the chance of studying your methods

suspect_architecture.value: Limited suspect pool established - wooden-legged man with distinctive voice, Lal Rao the butler as confederate, focus on identifying specific individuals rather than broad cast
suspect_architecture.quote: a brown, monkey-faced chap that's called more'n once for my old man

red_herring.value: Aurora launch description misdirection - Holmes deliberately gets details wrong (green with yellow line vs black with red streaks) to test witness reliability
red_herring.quote: She's not that old green launch with a yellow line, very broad in the beam?

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence shared with reader - wooden leg sound on stones, distinctive voice characteristics, launch visual details all presented to reader when Holmes receives them
clue_fairness.quote: I could hear the wooden leg clackin' on the stones.

social_world.value: Victorian class dynamics - Mrs. Smith's deference to Holmes's authority, working-class waterman culture, Holmes's casual manipulation of lower-class informants
social_world.quote: never to let them think that their information can be of the slightest importance to you

reveal_mechanic.value: Deductive reasoning demonstration - Holmes explains his interrogation technique and shows how he extracts reliable information through strategic deception
reveal_mechanic.quote: The main thing with people of that sort... is never to let them think that their information can be of the slightest importance

investigator_wound.value: Holmes's cocaine dependency and emotional detachment - his chemical escape from reality and stated opposition to emotional engagement conflicts with human connection
investigator_wound.quote: love is an emotional thing, and whatever is emotional is opposed to that true cold reason

_meta.title: ebook sir arthur conan doyle the sign of the four
